activities_projects.title: Identifying Speakers
activities_projects.objective: Distinguish between Amanda and the parent in the poem.
steps: Read the poem carefully.
Underline lines spoken by the parent.
Underline Amanda’s thoughts written in brackets.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students can identify who is speaking in each part of the poem.

activities_projects.title: Amanda’s Imaginations
steps: List the imaginary characters Amanda imagines herself as.
Write one sentence about why she wants to be each character.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct listing and explanation of characters.

activities_projects.title: Finding Lines About Freedom
steps: Find lines showing Amanda wants freedom or dislikes restrictions.
Write 2–3 sentences explaining these feelings.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students understand the main theme of freedom in the poem.
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy in identifying lines and clarity of explanation.

activities_projects.title: Counting Repetition
activities_projects.objective: Notice repeated use of Amanda’s name.
steps: Count how many times 'Amanda' appears in the poem.
Write one sentence about why the poet repeats her name.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students observe poetic techniques like repetition.

activities_projects.title: Describing Amanda
steps: List words that describe Amanda (e.g., imaginative, sensitive).
Write 3–4 sentences describing her character.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students can describe a character using text clues.

activities_projects.title: Role Play – Parent and Amanda
steps: One student acts as Amanda and another as the parent.
Read lines aloud with expression and emotion.
Discuss how Amanda feels in each scene.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice expressive reading and empathy.

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Activity
activities_projects.objective: Learn meanings of new words from the poem.
steps: Identify 4–5 new words from the poem.
Write their meanings and use them in simple sentences.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students enhance vocabulary using context clues.
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct meaning and usage in sentences.

activities_projects.title: Poem’s Message
activities_projects.objective: Understand the central idea of the poem.
steps: Write 4–5 sentences explaining what the poet wants to convey about Amanda and children.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students grasp the main message of the poem.

activities_projects.title: Drawing Amanda’s Imagination
steps: Pick one imaginary scene (e.g., mermaid, orphan, Rapunzel).
Draw the scene and write one line describing it.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students connect imagination with visual expression.
activities_projects.evaluation: Creativity and connection with the poem.

activities_projects.title: Writing a Short Summary
activities_projects.objective: Summarize the poem in simple words.
steps: Write 5–6 sentences summarizing the poem.
Include Amanda’s feelings and the parent’s instructions.
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Students practice summarizing and understanding key ideas.
